text:
WHOLE BODY FDG-PET/CT WITH DIAGNOSTIC CT
INDICATION FOR STUDY: Initial oncologic staging. 62-year-old female with newly diagnosed left upper lobe lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR L858R-mutant, confirmed by CT-guided core needle biopsy. Study requested for whole-body metabolic staging prior to treatment planning.

TECHNIQUE: Following a fasting period of at least 6 hours, the patient was administered 12.4 mCi of F-18 fluorodeoxyglucose (FDG) intravenously. Blood glucose at time of injection was 94 mg/dL. Images were acquired approximately 62 minutes after injection from the vertex to the mid-thighs using a dedicated PET/CT system (Siemens Biograph mCT). Attenuation correction was performed using low-dose CT. A separate diagnostic CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was acquired with intravenous iodinated contrast (Omnipaque 350, 100 mL, portal venous phase). Oral contrast was not administered. Images were reviewed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes with fused PET/CT display. Maximum intensity projection (MIP) images reviewed.

COMPARISON: No prior imaging available for comparison.

---

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK:
Limited non-diagnostic PET/CT head views are included in the whole-body acquisition; dedicated brain MRI is recommended for CNS staging and is not fully assessed on this study. No focal area of markedly abnormal FDG uptake is identified in the visualized intracranial structures. No cervical or supraclavicular nodal hypermetabolism is identified. No pathologically enlarged cervical lymph nodes by CT morphology.

THORAX:

Primary Lesion:
There is a spiculated, lobulated mass in the posterior segment of the left upper lobe measuring approximately 3.8 x 2.9 cm in axial dimension. The mass demonstrates intense, heterogeneous FDG avidity with a peak standardized uptake value (SUVmax) of 12.4. The margins are irregular with associated pleural tethering noted along the posterior pleural surface. A distinct satellite nodule is identified in the anterior segment of the left upper lobe, measuring 1.2 cm in greatest dimension, with moderate FDG uptake (SUVmax 6.8). This satellite nodule is anatomically separate from the primary mass and does not appear to represent a separate primary. No endobronchial lesion is identified. No chest wall or rib involvement.

Mediastinal and Hilar Lymph Nodes:
Multiple hypermetabolic lymph nodes are identified in the mediastinum and hila bilaterally.

- Right paratracheal region (station 4R): A cluster of enlarged, FDG-avid lymph nodes is present, with the dominant node measuring approximately 1.9 cm in short axis. SUVmax 9.1. The contralateral (right-sided) involvement in the setting of a left-sided primary constitutes N3 disease.

- Subcarinal region (station 7): An enlarged lymph node measures 1.4 cm in short axis with SUVmax 7.3, consistent with active nodal metastatic involvement.

- Left hilum (station 10L/11L): Mildly enlarged hilar nodal soft tissue demonstrates FDG avidity with SUVmax 5.2, compatible with nodal disease in the ipsilateral hilum.

- Left lower paratracheal region (station 4L): Borderline nodal tissue with mildly increased tracer uptake, below threshold for definitive involvement; cannot exclude early involvement.

- Aortopulmonary window (station 5): No discrete hypermetabolic node identified; subcentimeter soft tissue present without convincing uptake above background.

Lungs (remainder):
No additional pulmonary nodules with significant FDG uptake are identified beyond the primary lesion and satellite nodule described above. No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax.

HEART AND GREAT VESSELS:
Normal cardiac FDG distribution with physiologic blood pool activity. No pericardial effusion. Aorta and great vessels appear patent without atherosclerotic aneurysm. Mild diffuse atherosclerotic calcification of the thoracic aorta.

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:

Liver: The liver is normal in size and morphology. No focal hepatic lesion with pathologic FDG uptake identified. No biliary ductal dilatation.

Spleen: Normal size. No focal splenic lesion.

Pancreas: No focal pancreatic lesion or ductal dilation.

Adrenal glands: Both adrenal glands appear normal in size and morphology on CT. No focal adrenal mass or FDG uptake above background/physiologic adrenal activity.

Kidneys: Normal bilateral renal morphology with physiologic FDG excretion into the collecting systems. No renal mass. No hydronephrosis.

Gastrointestinal tract: Physiologic bowel FDG activity noted diffusely. No focal area of abnormal bowel uptake to suggest a metachronous GI primary or bowel metastasis. No bowel wall thickening on CT.

Retroperitoneum and abdominal/pelvic lymph nodes: No retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or pelvic lymphadenopathy. No pathologically enlarged nodes by size or metabolic criteria.

Bladder: Physiologic FDG excretion into the bladder. No bladder wall thickening or mass.

Uterus and adnexa (as visualized): No suspicious pelvic mass or FDG-avid pelvic lesion.

BONE:
Skeletal survey on PET and CT reviewed throughout the imaged skeleton. No focal lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion with associated abnormal FDG uptake to suggest osseous metastatic disease. Degenerative changes noted at the lower thoracic and lumbar spine (marginal osteophytes, mild disc space narrowing at L4-5 and L5-S1). No suspicious bone marrow uptake pattern.

SOFT TISSUES:
No subcutaneous or intramuscular soft tissue mass with abnormal FDG uptake identified. No pathologically enlarged superficial lymph nodes. No chest wall mass.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Intensely hypermetabolic left upper lobe mass (SUVmax 12.4, 3.8 x 2.9 cm) consistent with the known primary lung adenocarcinoma, accompanied by a metabolically active satellite nodule in the left upper lobe (SUVmax 6.8, 1.2 cm). The satellite nodule is classified within the same lobe (T3 by AJCC 8th edition criteria).

2. Hypermetabolic contralateral right paratracheal (station 4R) lymphadenopathy (dominant node 1.9 cm, SUVmax 9.1) and subcarinal (station 7) nodal disease (1.4 cm, SUVmax 7.3), representing contralateral mediastinal involvement. Ipsilateral left hilar nodal involvement (SUVmax 5.2). This nodal distribution is consistent with N3 disease.

3. No evidence of distant hematogenous metastatic disease in the imaged thorax, abdomen, or pelvis. No hepatic, adrenal, osseous, or soft tissue metastases identified.

4. No convincing evidence of FDG-avid intracranial disease on the limited head views; dedicated brain MRI with contrast is strongly recommended for complete CNS staging given the N3 nodal burden and EGFR-mutant histology.

5. Overall staging impression: Disease appears locoregionally advanced, consistent with Stage IIIB non-small cell lung cancer (T3 N3 M0, AJCC 8th edition), with no distant metastatic sites identified on this examination.

This study was reviewed and interpreted by the attending nuclear medicine physician. Correlation with clinical findings, tissue diagnosis, and multidisciplinary tumor board assessment is recommended.

ATTENDING NUCLEAR MEDICINE PHYSICIAN: [SIGNATURE ON FILE]
FACILITY: [INSTITUTION REDACTED]
